remember: Students will be able to recall the theme and tone of the poem 'Aunt Jennifer's Tigers'
Students will be able to identify the speaker and audience in the poem
Students will be able to list the imagery and symbolism used in the poem
understand: Students will be able to explain the significance of the tigers in the poem
Students will be able to describe the speaker's attitude towards Aunt Jennifer's life
Students will be able to analyze the contrast between Aunt Jennifer's life and her art
apply: Students will be able to analyze the use of language and imagery to convey the theme
Students will be able to evaluate the speaker's perspective on Aunt Jennifer's life
Students will be able to make inferences about Aunt Jennifer's life based on the poem
analyze: Students will be able to identify and explain the poet's use of irony in the poem
Students will be able to analyze the speaker's use of symbolism to convey the theme
Students will be able to compare and contrast the poem with other literary works

ncert_objectives: As the poem suggests that Aunt Jennifer's life was limited and restricted, students will be able to understand the theme of the poem.
The poem highlights the significance of art in one's life, students will be able to understand the importance of art in the poem.
board_exam_objectives: Identify and explain the theme and tone of the poem
Describe the speaker's attitude towards Aunt Jennifer's life
Explain the significance of the tigers in the poem
Compare and contrast the poem with other literary works
